title: Expressing Support for the President's Actions to Safeguard National Security and Eliminate Threats from Foreign Terrorist Organizations.
plain_summary: This resolution shows that the House of Representatives agrees with President Trump's actions to fight terrorism in early 2025. The President labeled certain dangerous criminal groups as terrorist organizations and worked to remove their members from the United States. The House supports using a law called the Alien Enemies Act to arrest and send away foreign terrorists. Some members of a group called Tren de Aragua were sent to El Salvador to be put in prison. The resolution says these actions help keep America safe from terrorist threats.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution expresses support for the President's actions to protect national security and eliminate threats from foreign terrorist organizations.

sections.title: Background Information
sections.text: On January twentieth, two thousand twenty five, President Trump signed Executive Order fourteen one five seven. This order labeled certain criminal groups called cartels and other organizations as Foreign Terrorist Organizations (groups from other countries that commit acts of terrorism) and Specially Designated Global Terrorists (people around the world who are marked as terrorists by the US government). The order specifically named a group called Tren de Aragua as a Foreign Terrorist Organization. This group has been told to attack US law enforcement officers. In early February two thousand twenty five, President Bukele of El Salvador met with Secretary of State Marco Rubio. They agreed that El Salvador would imprison members of Tren de Aragua on behalf of the United States. On March fifteenth, two thousand twenty five, President Trump used a law called the Alien Enemies Act because Tren de Aragua had entered the United States. On the same day, two airplanes carrying non-citizen members of Tren de Aragua and another gang called MS-thirteen left US airspace. On March sixteenth, two thousand twenty five, El Salvador President Nayib Bukele received these two flights and immediately moved the terrorists into El Salvador's Terrorism Confinement Center for detention.

sections.title: House of Representatives Actions
sections.text: The House of Representatives supports the President's action to remove foreign terrorists using the Alien Enemies Act, which is found in section fifty of the United States Code section twenty one and following sections. The House affirms that as Commander in Chief, the President has the executive power to detain, deport, or restrict non-citizens who belong to a Foreign Terrorist Organization. The House endorses the complete elimination of these organizations from the United States to protect the nation's territory, safety, and security.
